name: JourneyBook
migrate_command: dotnet ef migrations add <Name> -p dotnet/JourneyBook.Infrastructure -s apps/api

notes: Monorepo: pnpm workspaces + TS project references (atlas-core, map-sources, pdf-client, render-cli, ui). Build with `tsc -b`; engine modules import from the leaf ./model.js, NOT the barrel ./index.js (avoids an init cycle).

Backend: .NET 10 Clean Architecture (Domain <- Application <- Infrastructure <- Api). EF Core 10 + Npgsql + NetTopologySuite (PostGIS). Migrations project is dotnet/JourneyBook.Infrastructure with apps/api as startup.
Backend integration tests use Testcontainers.PostgreSql (image postgis/postgis:16-3.4) + Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing via PostgisApiFactory - they REQUIRE a running Docker daemon.
ADR 0004: the TS atlas-core engine is the single source of truth for geometry (projection/grid/scale/validation). Do NOT reimplement projection math in C#; the API persists metadata only.

Backend feature pattern (from Stage 2B): DTOs + I<X>Service (Application) -> <X>Service over JourneyBookDbContext (Infrastructure, registered in AddInfrastructure) -> minimal-API endpoints in apps/api/Endpoints/<X>Endpoints.cs -> integration test via PostgisApiFactory. Build geometry with NtsGeometryServices.Instance.CreateGeometryFactory(4326).
TS package tests use vitest; *.test.ts is excluded from the tsc build (exclude in tsconfig).

dev_env_prerequisites.name: docker
dev_env_prerequisites.description: Docker daemon for PostGIS Testcontainers backend integration tests

idempotency_env_fail_patterns: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon
